data_quality_note: scheduled run: free sources only (HN/GitHub/Google News/Substack), no X data

items.title: Study extracted system prompts from Fable 5, Opus 4.8, and Claude Code to calibrate your own agent design
items.why_useful: A growing collection of extracted system prompts from Anthropic Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Claude Code, and Claude Design alongside OpenAI, Google, and xAI models. Studying these production-grade prompts reveals how major labs structure constraints, format instructions, and guide model behavior—directly applicable when designing your own system prompts for agents. Engagement matured to 41,954 stars and 6,958 forks, the highest in this run.
items.author: asgeirtj

items.title: architect-loop: Claude Fable 5 as architect, Codex as builder, repo as memory
items.why_useful: A research-backed Claude Code skill that assigns Claude Fable 5 the architect role—planning, decomposing, reviewing—while delegating implementation to GPT-5.5 Codex, using the git repo as shared persistent memory. This cross-vendor division of labour is a directly transferable pattern: use the strongest planning model for design decisions and a fast builder for execution, with version control as the shared state layer. Engagement matured from 218 to 292 stars confirming ongoing adoption.
items.author: DanMcInerney

items.title: fable-mode: activate Fable-style planning, sub-agent delegation, and self-verification in any Claude Code project
items.why_useful: A Claude skill that activates Fable-style agentic behavior: explicit multi-stage planning, sub-agent delegation, and self-verification as a first-class pattern. Any team that wants to bring the Fable 5 orchestration model to their existing Claude Code setup can drop this skill in without modifying their core agent logic. 137 stars on a brand-new repo demonstrates rapid community uptake for the pattern.
items.author: mrtooher

items.title: Curated awesome list of Claude Fable 5 use cases with benchmark evidence
items.why_useful: A curated catalogue of Claude Fable 5 use cases organized by category, covering tutorials, integrations, demos, and benchmark evidence with source links. Useful as a discovery map when scoping what has been built and validated before committing to a new direction. Engagement matured from 168 to 183 stars and 44 forks since last tracked.
items.author: Anil-matcha

items.title: open-cowork: open-source computer-use agents for browser automation and desktop control
items.why_useful: An open-source alternative to Claude Co-Work, built with Claude Fable 5, providing autonomous computer-use agents for browser automation, desktop control, and agentic workflows. Delivers a self-hostable foundation for anyone building RPA or autonomous desktop-task pipelines without a cloud dependency. 27 stars and 9 forks on day one indicate early practical interest.
items.author: coasty-ai

items.title: value-for-fable: Fable 5-quality outputs at Sonnet-tier cost via smart model routing
items.why_useful: A Claude Code project that demonstrates how to achieve Fable 5-level output quality at Sonnet-tier pricing by building a cost-efficient model routing and operation layer. The pattern is directly transferable to any team running Claude Code continuously that wants to reduce token spend without sacrificing output quality. 41 stars and 9 forks on a brand-new repo confirm early real-world interest.
items.author: itsinseong
